mechanism_of_action: Biguanide that decreases hepatic glucose production, decreases intestinal absorption of glucose, and improves insulin sensitivity by increasing peripheral glucose uptake and utilization. Activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK).

metabolism.elimination: Renal (90% unchanged in urine within 24 hours)

special_populations.renal_impairment: Contraindicated if eGFR <30. Reduce dose if eGFR 30-45. Risk of lactic acidosis.
special_populations.hepatic_impairment: Avoid in severe hepatic impairment — increased lactic acidosis risk
special_populations.elderly: Monitor renal function closely. Titrate dose carefully.
special_populations.pregnancy: Generally considered safe; Category B. Increasingly used for gestational diabetes.

pharmacokinetics.onset_of_action: Within days (glucose lowering); weeks for HbA1c reduction

monitoring_parameters: Renal function (eGFR) at baseline and annually
black_box_warnings: Lactic acidosis (rare but potentially fatal, especially with renal impairment)
